ground_truth.title: Herding elephants: Lessons learned from sharding Postgres at Notion
ground_truth.author: Garrett Fidalgo
ground_truth.main_content: With an effort to make Notion faster and more reliable for years to come — we migrated Notion's PostgreSQL monolith into a horizontally-partitioned database fleet.
with: Herding elephants: Lessons learned from sharding Postgres at Notion
With an effort to make Notion faster and more reliable for years to come
We migrated Notion's PostgreSQL monolith into a horizontally-partitioned database fleet
A hand-drawn diagram depicts one cylindrical database, labeled Monolith
An illustration of Notion windows moving into sharding databases

items.alt: A hand-drawn diagram depicts one cylindrical database, labeled "Monolith", containing colored tables block, space, and comment. Below the monolith, arrows point to three smaller databases, each containing three rows of block, space, and comment tables. The smaller databases are labeled to indicate that there are 32 physical databases in total, with each database containing 15 logical shards.
items.caption: A hand-drawn diagram depicting a cylindrical database labeled 'Monolith'

items.alt: An illustration of Notion windows moving into sharding databases.
items.caption: An illustration showing Notion windows moving into sharding databases
